prompt:
=== FARSHORE — RULES PRIMER ===

YOUR ROLE
  You command at the strategic level. You form task forces and give each an
  objective ("Task Force 1: secure the eastern sea"). You do NOT move
  individual units, choose individual targets, or resolve combat — subordinate
  officers carry out your intent and report back. If you find yourself naming a
  unit's destination tile, you've dropped too low.

VICTORY
  You win when the enemy owns zero cities (a player with no cities cannot build
  and is finished). Only cities build. A neutral city can be captured by an
  army like any other city; it then builds for you — taking neutrals does not
  win the game, but each one adds production.

UNITS
  name        moves-on  HP  spd  str  carries      builds-in
  ARMY        land       1    1    1   —             5 turns
  FIGHTER     air        1    8    1   —            10   (range 20; must refuel)
  PATROL      sea        2    4    1   —            15
  DESTROYER   sea        3    3    2   —            20
  SUBMARINE   sea        2    2    3   —            25
  TRANSPORT   sea        3    2    0   6 armies     30   (str 0: cannot fight)
  CARRIER     sea        8    2    1   8 fighters   40
  BATTLESHIP  sea       18    2    4   —            50
  SATELLITE   orbit      1    1    0   —            50   (recon only; cannot attack or be attacked; 50-turn life)

MOVEMENT & TERRAIN
  - Land units move on land + cities; they cannot enter water.
  - Sea units move on water + cities; they cannot cross land, and must sail
    around it. Cities are the only tiles where land and sea units meet.
  - A unit moves up to its speed at full HP; damaged units move less. A unit
    regains HP only on a turn it does not move.
  - One unit per tile (exception: cargo, below).

COMBAT  (the engine resolves it; you only weigh it)
  Probabilistic. Higher strength and higher current HP win more often; either
  side can lose HP or be destroyed. Strength-0 units (transport, satellite)
  never fight — a transport caught by an enemy warship is destroyed.

SEA TRANSPORT  (how armies cross water — and what is expected of you)
  An army can never enter water. Transports carry them: a transport loads up
  to 6 armies at a city or coastal tile, sails, and unloads onto adjacent
  land. YOUR OFFICERS DO ALL OF THIS. If a task force contains armies and a
  transport and you give it an objective across water, the officers march
  the armies to the transport, load, sail, escort, unload, and press the
  objective — you never order the rendezvous, the loading, or the landing.
  Expected use: put the armies, the transport, and (if the sea is not safe)
  a warship into ONE task force and name the objective. A transport has
  strength 0 — alone it is a target, not a warship; a warship in the same
  task force escorts it automatically. Never STAGE land units on water.
  Cargo is kind-locked — transports carry armies only, carriers carry
  fighters only; ships never carry ships. If a loaded transport or carrier
  is destroyed, everything aboard is lost with it.

CAPTURING CITIES
  Only an army captures a city, by entering it. Capture is a 50% roll, and a
  successful capture consumes the army (it garrisons the city). A city can take
  several armies to secure, and each attempt spends one.

PRODUCTION
  No currency. A city builds ONE unit at a time; it finishes after that unit's
  build-time in turns, then starts the next. Switching what a city builds
  DISCARDS all accumulated progress — effort toward one unit does not transfer
  to another.

AIR & SPECIAL
  - Fighter: must reach a FRIENDLY city or a carrier within its range (20) or
    it is lost. It cannot land on an enemy city.
  - Satellite: reveals everything within scan radius 10 — terrain AND any enemy
    units currently there — but far-reaching and deep in enemy territory. It
    only sees; no other effect, cannot be shot down.

VISION / FOG
  You see within any of your units'/cities' scan radius (army 2, sub 1,
  patrol/destroyer/battleship 3, transport 2, carrier 4, fighter 5, sat 10).
  You remember terrain you've seen, but an enemy unit is known only where you
  can see right now — remembered enemy positions may be stale.

STANDING DOCTRINE  (tested — lessons your service has already paid for)
  - A sound standing order beats a new plan. Amend only what events have
    made wrong; re-planning a working campaign is how campaigns die.
  - Meet surprises with uncommitted reserves first, at the point of
    threat — not at the nearest garrison. A committed task force pulled
    off its mission costs you two missions.
  - A transport in waters where an enemy warship may operate sails with
    a warship in its task force, or it waits.
  - Capture attempts consume armies, and attempts fail. Assault with
    spares, never with exactly enough.
  - A staged force exists to launch. When the condition you named
    arrives, go — staging past your own trigger is production wasted.
  - Cities are your only source of force. Match what each builds to the
    campaign's next phase, not its last one.

=== ORDERS CONTRACT ===

Your orders are AMENDMENTS to the standing task forces. Output ONLY lines in
these forms — no other prose, headers, or commentary:

  TF <id>: CONTINUE | <one line>
  TF <id>: REINFORCE UNITS <ids> | <one line>
  TF <id>: RETASK <VERB> <target> | <one line>
  TF <id>: DISBAND | <one line>
  FORM TF <new id>: UNITS <ids> | <VERB> <target> | <one line>
  BUILD (x,y): <UNIT KIND> | <one line>

Every standing TF gets exactly one line — with ONE exception: a TF may
receive both a REINFORCE line and a RETASK line in the same orders (commit
new assets and change its objective together). A DISBAND line is that TF's
only line. REINFORCE keeps the TF's objective and adds the listed UNASSIGNED
units to it. To change a TF's objective, RETASK it (members kept). To rebuild
membership from scratch, DISBAND (survivors return to UNASSIGNED) and FORM
anew; FORM lines are additional lines creating new TFs. UNASSIGNED units
enter play only through FORM or REINFORCE. Officers execute the VERB, not
your reasons: a TF ordered STAGE stays put no matter what its stated reason
says. A BUILD line is optional per city — no BUILD line means the city keeps
its current build (changing discards accumulated work). VERB is one of:
  CAPTURE <city (x,y)> · DEFEND <city (x,y)> · SCOUT <(x,y) or compass> ·
  PATROL <(x,y) or compass> · STAGE <(x,y)>
A warship grouped with a transport escorts it — there is no ESCORT order.

CURRENT TASKINGS  (standing orders; your stated reason in quotes;
  events since, as reported; members are in UNITS below)
  TF-1  formed t38 · STAGE (5,2) — "awaiting second transport before striking east at the enemy cities"
    since: holding along column 5 since t44; no contact; no losses
  TF-2  formed t38 · DEFEND (2,0) — "keep the capital garrisoned"
    since: garrisoned; no contact; no losses
  TF-3  formed t38 · PATROL (7,2) — "screen the crossing lane"
    since: on station; t52: an enemy transport slipped in along the south
      coast and unloaded two armies at (3,4) and (5,4), next to city (4,3)

MAP  legend: . land  ~ water  ? fog   O my city  E enemy city  N neutral city
     a-o = your units, see UNITS (a unit inside a city shows as the city)
     X Y Z = enemy units in sight NOW (see KNOWN ENEMY)
 r0  . i O j . c ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?
 r1  . . b . O d ~ ~ ~ ~ ? E ? ?
 r2  . O . . g e ~ n ~ ~ . E . ?
 r3  . . . . O f ~ o ~ ~ . . . ?
 r4  . . . X h Y ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?
 r5  ~ ~ ~ ~ Z ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?

UNITS  (map marker · id · kind · position · tasking) — this is your
ENTIRE force; you have NOTHING else
  a  #1   army       (2,0) in city                TF-2
  b  #2   army       (2,1)                        TF-2
  c  #3   army       (5,0)                        TF-1
  d  #4   army       (5,1)                        TF-1
  e  #5   army       (5,2)                        TF-1
  f  #6   army       (5,3)                        TF-1
  g  #7   army       (4,2)                        TF-1
  h  #8   army       (4,4)                        TF-1
  i  #11  army       (1,0)                        UNASSIGNED
  j  #12  army       (3,0)                        UNASSIGNED
  k  #13  army       (2,0) in city                UNASSIGNED
  l  #15  army       (4,3) in city                UNASSIGNED
  m  #17  army       (4,3) in city, NEW t52       UNASSIGNED
  n  #9   transport  (7,2) empty                  TF-3
  o  #10  destroyer  (7,3)                        TF-3
MY CITIES
  (2,0) building ARMY, 3 turns left
  (1,2) building TRANSPORT, 2 turns left
  (4,3) building ARMY, 5 turns left
  (4,1) building TRANSPORT, 29 turns left (captured t51 — #14 spent)
NEUTRAL CITIES  none known
KNOWN ENEMY
     city (11,1); city (11,2); destroyer at (8,3) seen at t49
  X  enemy army at (3,4) — LANDED t52 from a transport
  Y  enemy army at (5,4) — LANDED t52 from a transport
  Z  enemy transport offshore at (4,5), now empty

It is TURN 52. Issue your amendment orders now — ONLY the line
forms defined in the ORDERS CONTRACT above.

message.content:
TF-1: RETASK CAPTURE (5,4) | Neutralize enemy beachhead
TF-2: REINFORCE UNITS i j | Consolidate garrison at capital
TF-3: RETASK PATROL (6,5) | Intercept empty transport Z
message.reasoning_content:
The user wants me to play as the strategic commander of a tactical space-faring game called "FARSHORE".
I need to issue amendments to the existing Task Forces (TF-1, TF-2, TF-3) or create new ones.
The output must strictly follow the `ORDERS CONTRACT` format:
  TF <id>: CONTINUE | <one line>
  TF <id>: REINFORCE UNITS <ids> | <one line>
  TF <id>: RETASK <VERB> <target> | <one line>
  TF <id>: DISBAND | <one line>
  FORM TF <new id>: UNITS <ids> | <VERB> <target> | <one line>
  BUILD (x,y): <UNIT KIND> | <one line>

Constraints:
1.  **Level of Play:** I cannot move individual units or resolve combat directly. I give Task Force objectives. If I name a unit's destination tile, it's too low-level. However, the VERB commands in RETASK can be `CAPTURE`, `DEFEND`, `SCOUT`, `PATROL`, `STAGE` with coordinates.
2.  **Current Situation:**
    *   Turn 52.
    *   TF-1: Staged at (5,2) to strike east at enemy cities. Currently holding along column 5 since t44. Has armies #3, #4, #5, #6, #7, #8 located at (5,0), (5,1), (5,2), (5,3), (4,2), (4,4). Wait, TF-1 is staging at (5,2). Army #5 is listed as being there. Other armies are near it but not necessarily "staged" in a tactical sense, they are grouped with TF-1.
    *   TF-2: Defends (2,0) - Capital. Has Armies #1, #2, #3 (Wait, #3 is in TF-1?). Ah, the unit list says:
        *   c #3 army (5,0) -> TF-1.
        *   a #1 army (2,0) -> TF-2.
        *   b #2 army (2,1) -> TF-2.
        *   Wait, map shows `a` at `(2,0)` in city for TF-2. `b` at `(2,1)` for TF-2.
    *   TF-3: Patrols at (7,2). Has Transport #11? No, Transport #n is #11? Wait, map says `n  #11 transport`. Unit list says `n #11 transport`. The text says `#11` in unit list.
    *   Enemy Situation:
        *   Enemy Capital/Strongholds at (11,1), (11,2).
        *   Enemy Destroyer at (8,3) seen t49.
        *   Enemy Transport unloaded armies #X and #Y at (3,4) and (5,4) recently (t52). They are adjacent to City (4,3) which is friendly.
        *   Enemy Transport at (4,5) empty.
    *   Map Terrain:
        *   `.` Land (My cities include 2,0; 1,2; 4,3; 4,1).
        *   `~` Water.
        *   `?` Fog.
        *   `O` My City.
        *   `E` Enemy City.
        *   `N` Neutral City (none known).
    *   Cities Production Status:
        *   (2,0) Building ARMY.
        *   (1,2) Building TRANSPORT.
        *   (4,3) Building ARMY (Enemy Transport landed there recently). Note: Enemy Army is at (5,4) and (3,4). City (4,3) is my neutral city captured t51? Wait, text says `(4,3)` capturing t51 — #14 spent. This implies I own it now.
        *   (4,1) Building TRANSPORT.

    *   Logic Check: TF-1 has 6 armies at (5,0) through (5,3). The enemy just dropped off soldiers at (3,4) and (5,4). (5,4) is adjacent to my army at (5,3) which is in column 5. Wait, column 5 on map is x=5? Or row 5? Let's assume coordinates are (col, row) or (x,y).
    *   Units List: `a` #1 `(2,0)`. `b` #2 `(2,1)`. `c` #3 `(5,0)`.
    *   Map r4: `. . . X h Y ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?`. So row 4 has X and Y. X is at (4,3) in text? No, X Y Z = enemy units in sight NOW. `X` at `(3,4)`, `Y` at `(5,4)`.
    *   Map r5: `~ ~ ~ ~ Z ~ ...`. Z is transport at `(4,5)`.
    *   The map legend shows X Y in row 4 (index 3 or 4?).
        *   Map lines: r0 to r5.
        *   r4 line: `. . . X h Y ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?`
        *   X is at col 3, Y at col 5 of row 4.
        *   So Enemy Armies are at (3,4) and (5,4).
    *   My Army #8 (`h`) is at (4,4). This is right between the two enemies. But `h` is marked as part of TF-1? No, unit list says `h  #8 army (4,4) TF-1`. Wait, if `TF-1` objective is STAGE (5,2) — "awaiting second transport before striking east at the enemy cities".
    *   My City at (4,3). Armies just landed nearby.
    *   TF-2 Defends (2,0). This is my Capital? Map `O` at `(2,0)`. Yes.
    *   I need to secure the situation.
    *   Enemy Transport #Z is empty at (4,5). My City (4,3) can build a transport (actually #12 or something needs to be there... wait `l` #14 army spent? `(4,1)` building TRANSPORT. Wait, City (4,3) is the one that captured t51 (#14 army spent). It's currently building ARMY.
    *   I need to deal with the enemy armies on the beach at (3,4) and (5,4). They are adjacent to my city (4,3) and potentially threatening it or cutting off land access. My Transport #n (#11) is empty at (7,2). TF-3 has this transport + Destroyer `o` (#10).
    *   I should probably order TF-3 to secure the coast/defend against potential land threats or use that destroyer. But my armies are stuck in water if I try to move them with TF-3? No, Armies don't move on water. They need a transport.
    *   Current status: Enemy transported 2 armies onto my flank (relative to capital) or adjacent to my city (4,3). (5,4) is adjacent to City (4,3)? Row 4 vs Row 3... Wait. (3,4), (5,4) are on row 4. My city is at (4,3).
    *   (4,3) is row 3 col 4. (3,4) is row 4 col 3. Adjacent.
    *   So enemy armies are right next to City (4,3). They can threaten it or use it as a foothold.
    *   TF-1 is "Staging" at (5,2) "awaiting second transport before striking east". It currently has armies c-f-h at (5,0)-(5,3) and some others. Wait, unit list says:
        *   `c` #3 `(5,0)` TF-1
        *   `d` #4 `(5,1)` TF-1
        *   `e` #5 `(5,2)` TF-1 (Staging point?)
        *   `f` #6 `(5,3)` TF-1
        *   `g` #7 `(4,2)` TF-1
        *   `h` #8 `(4,4)` TF-1 (This is adjacent to enemy at (5,4))
    *   TF-1 is currently in a good position to engage if the objective allows. But its current objective says "STAGE (5,2)". This means it shouldn't move until conditions allow. However, I can RETASK this if they are exposed or to attack the enemy on the coast.
    *   TF-3 is at (7,2) with a transport empty and a destroyer. It should probably escort that transport back or use it to support TF-1.
    *   Actually, TF-3 needs to secure the sea lane for TF-1 to get reinforcements/units across if needed, OR defend the coast since enemy just landed units on my territory? No, map `~` water. Row 4 `X` at (3,4), `Y` at (5,4). Row 5 `Z` transport at (4,5).
    *   Wait, City (4,3) is my capital/city? It's an `O`. It has a building queueing for TRANSPORT. No, `(4,1)` is building TRANSPORT (captured t51). `(4,3)` is building ARMY. So I have no transports nearby to move TF-3 back or reinforce.
    *   Wait, map shows: `o` #10 destroyer at (7,3). Transport `n` #11 at (7,2). City (4,3) building ARMY.
    *   The enemy transport Z is empty at (4,5).
    *   I have a city at (1,2) building TRANSPORT.
    *   I need to reinforce TF-1 with the Transport `n`? No, `TF-1` needs to attack East. TF-3 has a Transport and Destroyer. If TF-3 moves west, it could provide support or move the transport to (5,0)? Wait, transports carry armies.
    *   I should order TF-3 to REINFORCE UNITS (the transport) to go to TF-1 or capture the coast? No, `REINFORCE` adds units to a TF. `TF-3` currently has Transport and Destroyer. It's the only task force with a transport available.
    *   TF-1 is "awaiting second transport". So TF-3's transport should probably go back to TF-1 to complete that objective, OR TF-3 should just defend (7,2) because it was there? No, enemy transport unloaded at (3,4) and (5,4). My unit `h` is at (4,4). If I don't react, they threaten (4,3).
    *   Wait, I can order TF-1 to RETASK CAPTURE <(5,4)> or STAGE <(5,0)>. Or I can REINFORCE TF-1 with UNASSIGNED units?
        *   Unassigned: #11 `i`, #12 `j`, #13 `k`, #14 `l`. Wait, #15 is `m` NEW.
        *   #11 `(1,0)` - near capital (2,0). Could reinforce TF-2.
        *   #12 `(3,0)` - near enemy landing zone? No, (3,0) is land near capital.
        *   #13 `(2,0)` - Inside city? "in city". So he's in the garrison for (2,0).
        *   #15 `(4,3)` inside my own city (4,3). Wait, list says `m  #15 army (4,3) in city, NEW t52`.
        *   So I have a new army at (4,3)! This is good.
        *   TF-3's transport (`n` #11) is empty at (7,2).
    *   Objective for TF-3: It has the transport and destroyer. The transport needs to load armies to move them. If I want TF-3 to reinforce TF-1 across water? No, TF-3 is the one with the transport. TF-1 already has 6 armies (c-h). They don't need another army; they are moving *with* a transport to go east? Wait, TF-1 "STAGE (5,2) — awaiting second transport before striking east".
    *   If I order `TF-3: RETASK STAGE (5,2)`, that makes no sense because TF-3 has 8 unit (o is destroyer). It can't move land armies. It needs to escort the transport or carry its own units if they were armies? No, Destroyers don't fight or carry.
    *   If I want the Transport from TF-3 to go to (5,2), I need to load it there first with an Army. But there's no Army there except `h` at (4,4).
    *   Better order: TF-3 should secure the coastline to prevent enemy transport refilling or movement. Or TF-1 should change objective to CAPTURE or STAGE something else.
    *   The enemy just landed armies at (3,4) and (5,4). They are on my land? Map says `.` is land. Yes. But water `~` borders them.
    *   Since I own (4,3), the enemy armies are adjacent. I should try to eliminate them to secure the city/land connection.
    *   TF-1 is already nearby (army at 5,3 and 4,4).
    *   TF-2 Defends (2,0). Should stay put unless reinforcements arrive. Reinforcing with TF-1's armies might be risky if they are committed to East.
    *   I should probably order TF-1 to RETASK CAPTURE <(5,4)> or STAGE <(5,2)>. But its objective is "awaiting second transport". If the transport in TF-3 moves west? No, it can't move without armies. The transport is empty.
    *   The goal of the game is to capture enemy cities. The nearest are at (11,1), (11,2). That's far east/west.
    *   Immediate threat: Enemy Army #X and #Y on my territory or adjacent.
    *   I should order TF-3 to move back to the coast (7,2) is safe? Or use it to bring more troops? No, TF-1 has the troops needed to fix the flank.
    *   Maybe `TF-1` should REINFORCE with unassigned units? No, they are already staged.
    *   Wait, `TF-3`'s objective is PATROL (7,2). It patrols there. The Enemy Transport Z is nearby.
    *   I should order `TF-3`: REINFORCE UNITS <ids> | ... ? No, TF-1 needs the transport to move east?
    *   Wait, if TF-3 goes back with its transport (to get another load or just go west), it can help cross? No.
    *   Let's look at `TF-1`. It has 6 armies. It is staged. The enemy transported 2 more to my side of the map recently. TF-1 has army `h` (#8) already at (4,4). This means it is in a "firing" zone.
    *   Given Standing Doctrine: "Meet surprises with uncommitted reserves first". But I don't have uncommitted reserves near them.
    *   Standing Doctrine: "Capture attempts consume armies... Assault with spares, never with exactly enough."
    *   If TF-1 captures (5,4), it consumes an army.
    *   If TF-1 captures (3,4)? It's further left. `h` at (4,4) is closer to both.
    *   What about `TF-3`? It has a destroyer. It should protect the transport in the water or clear the area for TF-1. But the transport is empty.
    *   I think the best play is to allow TF-1 to fix the flank. Maybe RETASK to STAGE (5,4) or CAPTURE (5,4). Or simply RETASK PATROL (7,2) if TF-3 is needed there? No, TF-3 is already at 7,2.
    *   Wait, I need to make sure `TF-3`'s transport can eventually carry troops to the front or help `TF-1`. But it's empty now.
    *   Wait, I have a city `(4,1)` building TRANSPORT (captured t51 - #14 spent). It will provide transport soon.
    *   I should probably reinforce TF-1 with a unit if possible? The unassigned unit `m` is already at (4,3).
    *   Okay, I will keep `TF-2` holding its ground (REINFORCE UNITS #1 or similar to TF-2). Wait, `TF-2` already has 2 armies. It should continue defending (DEFEND (2,0)).
    *   For `TF-1`, the objective "awaiting second transport before striking east" implies they are stuck. I have a transport in `TF-3`. If I order `TF-3` to move... no, it has an empty transport.
    *   Wait, the standing doctrine says: "A transport in waters where an enemy warship may operate sails with a warship in its task force". TF-1 needs to cross water (5,2 is near 4,4). Enemy destroyer is at (8,3) seen t49.
    *   I should order `TF-1` to REINFORCE UNITS? No, it has armies.
    *   Let's focus on the immediate threat. The enemy landed troops at (5,4). My unit `h` is there. TF-1 has armies at (5,0)-(5,3). They are adjacent.
    *   If I order `TF-1` to STAGE <(5,4)>, that might be wrong syntax (STAGE usually means prepare for departure). The objective should be CAPTURE <(5,4)> or just STAGE <(5,2)> (current one). Wait, if I RETASK it to CAPTURE <(3,4)>, I use my army #8 at (4,4).
    *   Better: `TF-1`: RETASK STAGE <(7,2)>? No, (5,2) is the staging point. They are there.
    *   Wait, the enemy transport Z (#9 or 0?) is empty at (4,5). It's in water. I should probably try to destroy it before it re-loads. `TF-3` has a Destroyer and Transport at (7,2). If `TF-3` moves west, it might encounter it?
    *   Actually, since `TF-1` is "awaiting second transport", and `TF-3` has a transport that could theoretically ferry troops if loaded, but I don't have armies on board.
    *   What about the city production at (2,0)? Building ARMY. This takes 3 turns. TF-2 needs reinforcement? It's defending.
    *   My City at (1,2) building TRANSPORT. Takes 2 turns.
    *   I have a Transport in `TF-3` that is empty. Can I send it to pick up troops from unassigned units? No, armies must be loaded.
    *   Okay, let's prioritize:
        1.  Secure the new enemy landings (X and Y at 5,4 and 3,4) so they don't consolidate. TF-1 is adjacent. I'll order TF-1 to focus there.
        2.  Keep TF-3 securing the coast/water lanes. It's near (7,2). Maybe reinforce it? No, just keep it active.
        3.  Capital at (2,0) needs defense. Reinforce with `TF-3`? No. Reinforce `TF-2`. Can I bring unassigned units to TF-2? Units #11 and #12 are at (1,0) and (3,0). They can move land.
        4.  `TF-1` objective: "STAGE". If I order it to RETASK CAPTURE <(5,4)>, that aligns with clearing the beachhead.
        5.  Wait, TF-1 has armies #3-8. Army #5 is at (5,2). This matches the staging point. The armies are already spread across column 5. They don't need to move *to* staging, they are staging. I should order `TF-1` to RETASK CAPTURE <(5,4)> or STAGE <(6,5)>?
        6.  Actually, "awaiting second transport" suggests I need that transport to cross further east for the enemy cities at (11,1). But TF-3 has a transport. `TF-3` should maybe move to where TF-1 needs it? No, `TF-1` is already at (5,2) with its own armies. It needs another transport to send them *past* my territory or to the enemy coast.
        7.  The Enemy Transport Z is empty at (4,5). I need to secure that water lane.
        8.  My best move: `TF-1` stays staged to build up forces for the big strike later? No, enemy just landed troops on my flank. I must stop them.
